programme.title: Narcopolis
programme.desc: In the near future, Frank, a police officer, discovers that the legalization of all recreational drugs comes with a price.

programme.title: Official Secrets
programme.desc: A British intelligence officer turns into a whistleblower after learning about NSA espionage plans designed to ensure passage of a resolution of war against Iraq by the U.N. Security Council in 2003.

programme.title: Backcountry
programme.desc: A couple (Missy Peregrym, Jeff Roop) on a deep-wilderness hike become hopelessly lost within an aggressive black bear's territory.

programme.title: VFW
programme.desc: A typical night for veterans at a VFW turns into a battle for survival when a desperate teen runs into the bar. When a gang of punks come looking for her, the vets use every weapon at their disposal to protect themselves from an unrelenting attack.

programme.title: Prisoner X
programme.desc: A CIA agent arrives at a secret underground prison to interrogate a time-traveling terrorist believed to be behind a global civil war.

programme.title: Trench 11
programme.desc: In the final days of World War I, a soldier leads a team beneath the trenches to stop a German plot that could turn the tide of the entire war.

programme.title: Desolation
programme.desc: When a widow ventures into a remote wilderness area with her son and best friend to spread her husband's ashes, a mysterious hiker begins dogging them and forces them to confront their deepest fears.

programme.title: Devil's Gate
programme.desc: An FBI agent aims to solve a missing persons case in a rural area, but when she discovers that mysterious creatures may be responsible for the crime, she must tap into every reserve of her courage and smarts to survive.

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: While making an instructional video, hunters become the prey after they trespass on government land.

programme.title: Blood Glacier
programme.desc: Scientists discover that a red liquid leaking from a nearby glacier contains organisms that turn living hosts into deadly mutants.

programme.title: Lake of Death
programme.desc: Lillian and some friends travel back to the remote family cabin where her twin brother died the previous year. Soon, strange things start happening.

programme.title: The Numbers Station
programme.desc: Emerson, a disgraced CIA agent, guards Katherine, a code operator, in a top secret CIA installation in Britain. When heavily armed assailants lay siege to the compound, Emerson and Katherine have to stop the deadly plot before it's too late.

programme.title: American Fable
programme.desc: A young girl (Peyton Kennedy) learns that her beloved father is hiding a wealthy man in a silo to save their struggling farm.

programme.title: Centigrade
programme.desc: A man and his pregnant wife become trapped in their car when a storm buries them underneath layers of snow and ice. As supplies dwindle and temperatures plunge, the couple must battle the elements and hypothermia in a desperate fight for survival.

programme.title: Baskin
programme.desc: Policemen (Gorkem Kasal, Ergun Kuyucu) face a night of horror in a subterranean labyrinth after answering a distress call.

programme.title: I Trapped the Devil
programme.desc: Hoping for a joyful family reunion, Matt and his wife pay a surprise visit to the home of his estranged brother, Steve, to celebrate Christmas. To their shock, they soon learn that Steve has a hostage in his basement -- a man he claims is the devil.

programme.title: Extraterrestrial
programme.desc: Malevolent aliens interrupt five collegians' vacation at a remote woodland cabin.

programme.title: Into the Ashes
programme.desc: Nick is an ex-con who's adjusting to a new job, a new wife and some semblance of normalcy in rural Alabama. When vengeful former associates track him down, he's soon forced back into the desperate and violent life that he thought he left behind.

programme.desc: Things lurking on the periphery of people's lives lead to horrific conclusions.

programme.title: The Nazis: A Warning from History
programme.desc: Nearly one in five Poles died during World War II after the Nazi invasion in 1939 ushered in one of the most brutal episodes of ethnic cleansing the world has ever seen. This film destroys the myth that Poland's Nazi leaders were acting under detailed 'orders' and that the country's Nazi administration was a model of German efficiency. Arthur Greiser and Albert Forster, the two Nazi leaders charged with Germanising Poland, could not even agree on who should be Germanised. Featuring interviews with former Nazis, moving testimony from witnesses to SS atrocities in Poland, ethnic Germans and Polish Jews, the programme provides insights into how the Nazis' reign of terror in Poland was characterised by huge population upheavals, chaos, petty squabbles and sheer bloodlust.

programme.title: Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox journeys into a supermassive black hole - a monster that can destroy worlds and stop time, and which is forcing us to reassess our understanding of reality.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the second of the series semi-finals, two more teams of students face off for a place in the grand final. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. In the first of the Semi Finals, three lovers of the pastoral world of Radio 4's The Archers face new opponents. How will they respond to the threat of cavalier risk-taking from a team of confirmed Gamblers? They'll compete to draw together the connections between elements which at first glance seem utterly random, including purple dye and the Jules Rimet trophy.

programme.title: The Railway Children
programme.desc: 1904, England. When their father is mysteriously arrested, the Waterbury children and their mother downsize and move to the Yorkshire countryside. Bobbie, Phyllis and Peter discover the local steam railway, and on their adventures, they befriend the station porter, Mr Perks, and try to discover the truth about their father. Lionel Jeffries's beloved film version of ES Nesbit's classic book.

programme.title: Big Screen Britain: The Railway Children
programme.desc: Ben Fogle travels to West Yorkshire with actress Sally Thomsett to visit the locations of key scenes from the classic 1970 film The Railway Children.

programme.title: Close Encounters of the Third Kind
programme.desc: Out on a late night job alone, power repairman Roy Neary has an encounter with an alien spacecraft and becomes consumed with the desire to see it again. The government denies it, but other people have been seeing the UFOs and having strange experiences, too. Soon, Roy is on a one-way mission to expose the truth.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. In the first of the Semi Finals, three lovers of the pastoral world of Radio 4's The Archers face new opponents. How will they respond to the threat of cavalier risk-taking from a team of confirmed Gamblers? They'll compete to draw together the connections between elements which at first glance seem utterly random, including purple dye and the Jules Rimet trophy.

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: A No 1 album and multiple Grammy wins has put singing sensation Samara Joy on the map, establishing the young American artist as ‘a legend in the making’, regularly compared to Ella Fitzgerald and Sarah Vaughan. This much-anticipated Proms debut introduces audiences to her electric combination of youthful energy and old-soul musical style with classic songs and unique twists on timeless tunes, from bossa nova to the Great American Songbook. She salutes figures including Sarah Vaughan, Ella Fitzgerald, Duke Ellington, Billie Holiday, Charles Mingus and Oscar Peterson. Andi Oliver is the guide for an evening of charisma and musical glamour.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 21 January 2000 and featuring Pet Shop Boys, Donell Jones, Point Break, Missy 'Misdemeanor' Elliott, Eurythmics, Scooch, Justin and Manic Street Preachers.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 28 January 2000 and featuring Donell Jones, Sting feat Cheb Mami, Nu Generation, Super Furry Animals, Hi-Gate, Manic Street Preachers, Scanty Sandwich and Britney Spears.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Janice Long presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 3 July 1986. Featuring The Housemartins, Samantha Fox, Claire & Friends, Sly Fox, Gary Numan, Bucks Fizz and Wham!

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 6 July 1989 and featuring Sonia, Monie Love and The Beautiful South.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As Adam Frost’s brand new garden starts to take shape, it’s time to get some shrubs in the ground and construct a path. He also makes a start on planting up a potted herb garden and shares some ideas on what to grow against a wall, whether it be sunny or shady. At home in Devon, Carol Klein is mixing it up with flowers and edibles, where sweet peas and cosmos happily mingle with beetroot, basil and beans. And at West Dean Gardens in Chichester, Jamie Butterworth reveals the secrets of growing that border belle, the delphinium. In Birmingham, a lover of roses has created a garden full to brimming with beautiful blooms, while a gardener in Norwich shares her contemporary cottage garden that became a refuge when she needed it most.

programme.title: Words and Music
programme.desc: Legendary and newer US singer-songwriters - including Jackson Browne, Nanci Griffith and Loudon Wainwright - perform one of their key songs and explain how they came to write it.

programme.title: The Old Grey Whistle Test
programme.desc: Jackson Browne in a live concert from the BBC Television Theatre, London. Introduced by Bob Harris.

programme.title: The Late Show
programme.desc: A Late Show special from 1992, featuring US singer-songwriters Nanci Griffith, Rosanne Cash and Mary-Chapin Carpenter in an acoustic performance at New York's The Bottom Line club.

programme.title: BBC Four Sessions
programme.desc: In a programme first broadcast in 2005, Loudon Wainwright III plays tracks from his album Here Come the Choppers alongside such classics as One Man Guy and Hard Day on the Planet to an enthusiastic audience. Footage shot during his British tour that year is at once hilarious and moving, revealing a complex and talented character who has spent decades turning everyday personal matters into public art.

programme.title: The Whistle Test Years
programme.desc: Bob Harris introduces clips from 1975. Featuring performances by Little Feat, Ronnie Lane's Slim Chance, Richard & Linda Thompson, John Lennon, Billy Joel, Jackson Browne and Lynyrd Skynyrd.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 21 January 2000 and featuring Pet Shop Boys, Donell Jones, Point Break, Missy 'Misdemeanor' Elliott, Eurythmics, Scooch, Justin and Manic Street Preachers.

programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 28 January 2000 and featuring Donell Jones, Sting feat Cheb Mami, Nu Generation, Super Furry Animals, Hi-Gate, Manic Street Preachers, Scanty Sandwich and Britney Spears.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Janice Long presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 3 July 1986. Featuring The Housemartins, Samantha Fox, Claire & Friends, Sly Fox, Gary Numan, Bucks Fizz and Wham!

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 6 July 1989 and featuring Sonia, Monie Love and The Beautiful South.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A pride of lions has rewritten the rules - they have learnt to take down elephants.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: For most of Earth's history there was no life on land. But over 400 million years ago some tiny plants began an invasion from the water, closely followed by the first animals - the ancestors of millipedes and insects.

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose tells the story of one of the greatest ever sea adventures, which transformed Captain James Cook into a national hero and dramatically changed the course of history. Two and a half centuries later, Captain Cook is still a household name, but his achievements are often misunderstood, contrary to popular perception, he did not discover New Zealand and Australia. Intrepid Rose follows his journey down under and uncovers the real story of Captain Cook.

programme.title: Starter for 10
programme.desc: Comedy about Brian, a 1980s working-class boy stumbling romantically and academically through his first year at Bristol University. He tries to achieve his lifelong ambition to appear on TV quiz show University Challenge and falls in love with his teammate.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: When Hyacinth and Richard go to stay in a country cottage for a couple of days, events take several unexpected turns. Richard goes in search of a dog, and finds himself caught up with more than he bargained for.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Margaret joins the local amateur dramatic society and takes Victor to a Bergerac party. Meanwhile, Margaret's mother manages to get stuck in the bath with wallpaper paste.

programme.title: Woolly Mammoth: Secrets from the Ice
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts reveals the natural history of the most famous of ice age animals - the woolly mammoth. Mammoths have transfixed humans since the depths of the last ice age, when their herds roamed across what is now Europe and Asia. Although these curious members of the elephant family have been extinct for thousands of years, scientists can now paint an incredibly detailed picture of their lives thanks to whole carcasses that have been beautifully preserved in the Siberian permafrost. Alice meets the scientists who are using the latest genetic, chemical and molecular tests to reveal the adaptations that allowed mammoths to evolve from their origins in the tropics to surviving the extremes of Siberia. And in a dramatic end to the film, she helps unveil a brand new woolly mammoth carcass that may shed new light on our own ancestors' role in their extinction.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: For most of Earth's history there was no life on land. But over 400 million years ago some tiny plants began an invasion from the water, closely followed by the first animals - the ancestors of millipedes and insects.

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: As governments and businesses confront their reliance on US tech giants, AI Decoded explores whether Britain can build its own AI future - and how quickly it must act. Now, a sudden decision from Washington is sending shockwaves through the global AI industry, restricting access to some of America's most advanced systems - even for its allies. For UK companies, it raises a stark question: what happens when the technology you depend on is no longer available? Presenter Marc Cieslak is joined by Yang Li of East London startup Cosine, now racing to build a 'sovereign' British alternative as demand surges following the US clampdown. Bloomberg's Katrina Manson also joins to unpack the national security stakes, alongside co-host Stephanie Hare.

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Adrienne Murray meets the team using technology to preserve a historic warship in Sweden. Shiona McCallum has a round-up of the key innovations and trends on show at VivaTech in Paris. Suranjana Tewari heads behind the scenes of a driverless taxi service in Beijing. And as the summer music festival season gets underway, Andrew Rogers reviews some of the latest ear defender tech on the market.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: China's Greatest Treasures
programme.desc: Music and dance in China enjoy a fascinatingly long lineage dating back to at least 3000 BC. Ancient documents reveal countless forms of music and dance in connection with religious rituals, folk festivals and everyday life. Alastair explores some of the most iconic forms of ancient Chinese music and dance and also discovers how they are celebrated and kept alive today.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: To mark America's 250th anniversary, Talking Movies hears from leading figures in US media, film and culture who tell the BBC about some iconic American movies and what these films reveal about the country and the psyche of her people. American Psycho director Mary Harron talks about Martin Scorsese's The King of Comedy, while critic and film-maker Bilge Ebiri looks at Hitchcock's classic thriller North by Northwest. BBC News presenter Sumi Somaskanda reveals her passion for the prison drama The Shawshank Redemption and award-winning journalist Tim Teeman selects Steven Spielberg's Jaws. Talking Movies' host Tom Brook chooses the Robert Altman ensemble satire Nashville. Each contributor speaks about what their film choice means to them and how and why they feel it has become an American cinema classic. This special illustrates the American nation on the big screen and the influence it's had in bringing its movies to the world.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: A global, topical weekly show that lifts the hood on the entertainment and news business. The Media Show explores the companies and personalities who command our attention, and how our perception of the world is shaped by their actions. From the streaming giants who entertain us, to the news outlets and social media platforms that inform and enrage us, The Media Show explains how the industry really works.

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: On the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence, Dwayne Fields travels across the USA, exploring how the document's famous promise - the pursuit of happiness - is being lived today. In the second part of his adventure, Dwayne travels from the centre of the country's happiness industry in Las Vegas to its biggest Chinatown in San Francisco to explore what happiness means to Americans today.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: An accountant takes on three different full-time jobs with the NHS – and manages to get paid for all of them, even though he’s barely carrying out the work of one. Each is well paid in its own right, and he hasn’t declared that he’s working three at the same time. In fact, he’s barely working one, as he’s using the money to fund a luxury lifestyle of London hotels and extravagant days out. Investigators also discover the accountant has been taking on private clients and draining their bank accounts into his own. He’s even accused of romance fraud. In another case, undercover work exposes a benefit cheat’s lies. A woman has been claiming disability living allowance because she says she has multiple sclerosis. But after a tip-off, investigators from the Department for Work and Pensions launch a surveillance operation. She’s seen walking normally with no apparent disability. And her social media account shows a video of her on a zip wire. Investigators call her in for questioning.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: We see the culmination of an epic, six-and-a-half-year project in the West Midlands. Martel loves showing off properties in Scotland. The one she visits in Livingston may not be the finest example but it has potential. Dion is impressed by the space on offer in Leeds.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams saddle up for a race around the antiques stalls at Wetherby Racecourse. Danny Sebastian is holding the reins, alongside experts Caroline Hawley and Colin Young. The reds, George and Colin, have been best friends for 36 years, while the blues are couple Lu and Tak, both education consultants. Unsurprisingly, they spot an old school desk, but does it make the grade? Colin takes a shine to a set of silver spoons with a local connection, but is the deal sweet enough? And as the clock ticks down, the reds get sidetracked by a framed letter supposedly written by King George VI. Meanwhile, the blues go potty over vases in the hope of fulfilling Danny’s challenge.

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Spain take on Austria in Los Angeles as the 2026 FIFA World Cup round of 32 continues. Eighteen-year-old Lamine Yamal is the star attraction for Luis de la Fuente's side as they look to add the sport's biggest prize to their Euros triumph two years ago. Ralf Rangnick's Austria are hoping to back up their dramatic qualification from the group stage with what would be a seismic scalp in their first World Cup knockout game since 1982.

programme.title: How Are You? It's Alan (Partridge)
programme.desc: Hosting Saudi Radio alone in a Norwich business park prompts Alan to ask the question: is solitude healthy, or do we need other people? Can dogs or technology fill the void?

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Three-time quarter-finalists Switzerland meet Algeria in the round of 32 at the 2026 FIFA World Cup in Vancouver, Canada. The Swiss were unbeaten in their opening three games to top Group B, whilst Algeria drew with Austria in their final group match to squeak through to the knockout stage for the second time as one of the best third-placed sides.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Featuring an audacious asbestos dump posing a risk to public health and the environment. And the shopfitter who dodged £2.5 million in tax to splurge on luxury cars and holidays.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion meets a football fan in Liverpool who is keen to tailor his new property for visitors to Anfield. In South Shields, Martel is tiptoeing around the drips from the leaking roof. Jacqui is riding high in a tower block in Essex.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Can you get the measure of Christina Trevanion's mystery object? It has a metal base that supports a measuring stick, with a moveable plastic pump attached. Will the fairgoers in Exeter guess what it is? While they puzzle its purpose, antiques experts Gary Pe and Ben Cooper hunt for bargains with dad and daughter David and Ellie and partners Darren and Simon. Darren immediately spots a stall of shiny costume jewellery. Gary thinks its inspiration comes from the Middle East but doubts any of it is profitable. On the other team, Ellie takes a fancy to a pair of etched, cut-glass decanters, but as Ben explains, they don’t make the sort of money they used to, so they stroll on. One of our pairs is also tempted by a massive nut with an exotic name – until they see the price tag! After the fair, Christina and the teams head to Wiltshire to see whether their items will bring in the cash at auction. Tim Weeks is on the rostrum, but who is going home with the most money?

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: The 2026 FIFA World Cup round of 32 continues with Australia against Egypt in Dallas, with only two places separating the sides in the world rankings at the start of the tournament. The Socceroos have never gone beyond the last 16 but impressed in finishing behind co-hosts USA in their group. Egypt advanced past the opening stage for the first time. However, they have concerns over the fitness of their captain and talisman Mo Salah.

programme.title: How Are You? It's Alan (Partridge)
programme.desc: Can nature heal a troubled mind? Alone in the Peaks with only a selfie stick, Alan draws on the teat of Mother Nature and unlocks a heroic side some people doubted he ever had.

programme.title: Anna Haugh’s Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: Having grown up just two hours from Belfast, Anna is excited to visit the city for the first time. She meets international boxing champion Carl Frampton as he shows her around his home city. Anna learns about Veda bread, a unique malt loaf staple in Belfast, before cooking for tourists at the birthplace of the Titanic. Carl puts Anna through her paces in the boxing ring and brings her to one of the city’s last traditional bakeries, where she discovers the local sweet treat, Fifteens.

programme.title: Matthew Perry and the Ketamine Queen
programme.desc: Documentary investigating events leading up to the shocking death of Friends star Matthew Perry and the woman at the centre of the case, dubbed the 'Ketamine Queen' by US prosecutors. Jasveen Sangha, a drug dealer and dual US-UK citizen, has pleaded guilty to five federal charges in Los Angeles, including one count of distributing ketamine resulting in death or bodily injury. She faces up to 65 years in prison for supplying Perry with the drugs that led to his death. Sangha was one of five people charged in the case. Prosecutors described her home as a 'drug-selling emporium', where authorities found approximately 79 vials of ketamine and other drugs, including methamphetamine, cocaine and Xanax. Presented by Amber Haque, the film features exclusive, first-on-the-record interviews with Sangha's friends and examines her role in Perry’s death and Hollywood’s ketamine drug network.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Relive the 2022 World Cup final between Argentina and France.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Relive a thrilling World Cup final between France and Croatia.

programme.title: The TikTok Effect
programme.desc: What connects amateur sleuths turning up at crime scenes, anti-social behaviour in UK schools and riots in France? The answer, according to a BBC investigation, is that they are all examples of a TikTok 'frenzy'. Marianna Spring, the BBC’s disinformation correspondent, has spent the last year investigating harmful behaviour on TikTok spilling out into the real-world. She tracks down users, victims and former employees to expose how extremely high engagement on TikTok around some topics appears to be creating these 'frenzies' linked to behaviour previously seen as unacceptable.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: The wildlife action moves to the south coast of Australia as naturalist Steve Backshall and the Deadly 60 team take the plunge in the waters of the Pacific. Steve has heard that yellowfin tuna are super-fast lethal killers, but what makes them so special? He witnesses their missile-like streamlined bodies for himself and is left in no doubt as to their turbo-charged credentials when he dives with over a thousand of them. He then tests his own body's streamlined capabilities, with hilarious results. Strange alien-like giant cuttlefish also await Steve in the warm Pacific waters. It is then a short hop up the coast to try and track down perhaps one of the weirdest animals on Steve's list, the elusive duck-billed platypus.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bea’s behaviour leaves Honey concerned. Cindy clashes with Zoe over business rivalry. Walford celebrates Pride.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May set out to buy second-hand convertibles and discover some of the unusual pitfalls to watch out for when picking up a used car bargain. Jeremy is on the test track in the powerful Pagani Zonda R, whilst Simon Pegg is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: The Traitors US
programme.desc: A bloody banquet shakes the whole castle. Traitor trust teeters, and a gamer takes on a housewife. The mission means a trek to the epic Fyrish Monument, where one player will win a great power. Alliances are tested at the Round Table.

programme.title: The Traitors US
programme.desc: While the temperature rises between the Traitors, a strategic murder shakes the castle, alliances are tested, and a heated conversation makes for a simmering Round Table. One player has an impossible decision to make.

programme.title: Blink Twice
programme.desc: When tech billionaire Slater King meets cocktail waitress Frida at his fundraising gala, sparks fly. He invites her to join him and his friends on a dream vacation on his private island. Wild nights blend into sun-soaked days, and everyone is having a great time. No-one wants this trip to end, but as strange things start to happen, Frida begins to question what is real and what is not. There is something wrong with this place. She will have to uncover the truth if she wants to make it out of this party alive.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May set out to buy second-hand convertibles and discover some of the unusual pitfalls to watch out for when picking up a used car bargain. Jeremy is on the test track in the powerful Pagani Zonda R, whilst Simon Pegg is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Blackmailed: The Sextortion Killers
programme.desc: Reporter Tir Dhondy investigates the rise of sextortion scams targeting teenage boys on social media, including the case of 16-year-old victim Evan Boettler. Her journey leads to Nigeria, where she confronts the scammers taking part in these crimes. With unprecedented access and interviews with industry insiders, Dhondy exposes a disturbing online underworld and its devastating consequences.

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: How a Somerset high-flyer turned his charm and connections into tools of deception – conning his school friends and hundreds of others while living a life of glamour himself.

programme.title: Dance Passion
programme.desc: The UK’s dance artists, from classical ballet dancers and Strictly Come Dancing stars to choreographed skydivers and car-eographed drivers, race across the country to meet Lady G in Corey Baker’s thrilling new film. It rallies dancers, drones, precision drivers and a closed track - buckle up for dance like you’ve never seen it before. Corey Baker is known for his films featuring dance in striking and unexpected locations, including the award-winning Swan Lake Bath Ballet for BBC Arts, filmed in the bathtubs of elite ballet dancers around the world during lockdown. Part of Dance Passion, a celebration of dance from across the UK.

programme.title: Roald Dahl's Matilda the Musical
programme.desc: Musical based on Roald Dahl's classic book. Matilda Wormwood is a little girl with a sharp mind, a vivid imagination and the worst parents in the world. On meeting her inspirational teacher Miss Honey, Matilda begins conjuring her own fantastical tales.

programme.title: Ludwig
programme.desc: After a breakthrough finding a coded notebook, John decides to return to the police station to gather more information about his missing brother’s last case. Before John can make much progress, his thirst for all things cryptic sees him drawn to another grisly crime scene at a grand manor house. What looks like a missing person’s case turns into something far more sinister as John puts his puzzle mind to work. Back at home, a frustrated Lucy takes matters into her own hands and embarks on a fact-finding mission to the home of one of her husband’s ex-colleagues.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: After finding out Mal is banging the gym teacher, Amanda is spurred into action on the dating front herself. She accepts a date from Johannes – a property developer from South Africa who now lives in a penthouse in Wapping. He’s seen her ‘Senuous’ social media page and is keen to meet for a drink. Meanwhile, things are getting serious between Amanda’s daughter Georgie and Anne’s son Darius, much to Anne’s delight. Felicity seems to have taken a lover of her own, disappearing off for ‘lunches’ and buying luxury pyjamas, and Mal is worried that his new girlfriend might be a little too young for him.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda finds herself out of her comfort zone when Anne organises a camping trip. Amanda’s new boyfriend Johannes is a keen camper, so she’s forced to dig out her Hunter wellies and tag along – on the proviso that she can hire a camper van. What should be a wholesome weekend away ends up being a nightmare. Amanda realises that the VW Westphalia she's hired doesn’t have a toilet. So it’s either the communal drop loos, or she crosses her legs for the entire weekend. Meanwhile, Johannes rubs Mal up the wrong way, Anne gets hormonal, Fi embraces life under canvas, and Della has to deal with a disaster back at the restaurant.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: It’s the end-of-season awards do at Hounslow Eagles' clubhouse, and Amanda throws herself into the organisation of the prize-giving party. So, it's out with the buckets of Haribo and a keg of beer, and in with the black tie, churros and cocktails. Meanwhile, not only has her new boyfriend Johannes bought her a brand new Range Rover, he also makes her a generous offer – to move into his massive Wapping penthouse. Amanda is presented with a difficult choice. Does she return to her life of high ceilings and 4x4s, or does she fully commit to her SoHa family? While all this is going down, Georgie and Morten cement their friendship, Mal and Ned take over the decks, and Della has some big news to break to Fi.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda and the family head to Aunt Joan’s country house, but the plans change when Mal and Anne tag along, and she struggles to recreate the magical Christmases she spent there as a child. Anne is in despair at being separated from her family, and Felicity is increasingly irritated by her sister’s constant high spirits. Meanwhile, Mal sees a hidden photo that seems to reveal a deep family secret.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It’s games night, and Dickie has concocted 'an absolutely amazing' murder mystery evening full of twists and turns. Solving the murder should be easy, if he can keep it all on track.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: The gang learn that it’s Stewart’s birthday, but he doesn’t want them to make a big fuss out of it in case his grandmother or colleagues find out he’s gay. The queens come up with a cunning plan.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It’s Middlesbrough Pride! Mam is hosting the main stage and has booked a Girls Aloud drag tribute act. It ‘promises’ to be a banger.

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: When Miri Matteson returns home after eighteen years, can she integrate back into her old life? With a terrible event from her past hanging over her, it won’t be easy.

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: Miri sets about mending her friendship with Billy, after his terrible reaction to the truth of who she really is. She seems to have made progress until an encounter with his wife Anna takes a dark turn – and Miri winds up under the scrutiny of the local police. Meanwhile, having ended her relationship with Dom, Caroline is determined to relight the fire between her and Oscar. Unfortunately, no matter what she does and how hard he concentrates, Oscar’s body won’t respond to the attention. Could it have something to do with his inability to be honest with Caroline about her betrayal? Across town, Miri heads back to work at Nathan’s fish and chip shop, only to find that the shop is no more. Nathan is now running a food truck and, despite the positive spin he puts on it, it’s clear that hiring Miri was a terrible idea. Mandy is still trying to connect with Miri, who refuses to give her a second chance. But by the end of the day Miri is running out of friends and allies. Perhaps she will be forced to reconsider.

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: Miri wakes up in a police cell once again – this time charged with arson. Although a mystery alibi quickly clears her name, the community will take a little longer to accept her innocence. After he secures her release, Miri agrees to meet the mysterious Scandinavian man who’s been following her since her return. He claims to know why Lara was so angry with Miri on the night that she died. His conclusion is far-fetched, but is it as far from the truth as it might first seem? Disappointed, Miri returns home to find that the vandals have struck again. It may be Oscar’s birthday, but the Mattesons aren’t going to get a break. To make matters worse, Billy is now a target too. Hurt and angry, Miri is still determined to move on and be judged for the person she is now, not for the terrible thing she did many years ago. Things come to a head as Miri confronts the community at a meeting held to run her out of town. As the meeting erupts in to turmoil, secrets and lies are laid bare, and it turns out that Miri isn’t the only one with a dark past.

programme.title: Ellie & Natasia
programme.desc: Sketch show by Ellie White and Natasia Demetriou, including a pair of vaping dominatrixes and the Greek-Cypriot entrepreneurs making their own James Bond movie.

programme.title: Ellie & Natasia
programme.desc: Sketch show by Ellie White and Natasia Demetriou. The Brothers Pomodoro return to show off their new cookbook, and there’s a song about snakes.

programme.title: Ellie & Natasia

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: When an underwater base comes under attack, the Doctor and Clara must save the frightened crew and defeat an impossible threat. But what is behind these terrifying events? And can they really be haunted by ghosts?

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: On a remote army outpost, a fearsome alien warlord called the Fisher King sets in motion a twisted plan to ensure his own survival. The ripples will be felt around the universe. Is this chain of events inevitable? And can the Doctor do the unthinkable?

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Wendy paints a portrait of Lee's late father, Frank, and gives it to Lee and Lucy as a present. She is expecting them to love it, but that would require the painting to be not just competent but good. Once it’s hanging on their wall, Lee and Lucy must contrive to lose the painting without Wendy or Geoffrey noticing.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee accidentally sends a rude text about Anna to Lucy, he makes the stupid mistake of sending it to Anna herself. Lee and Lucy now face the impossible task of stealing Anna's phone and deleting the text before she reads it.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira receives a gift from an old friend, and it turns out to be a cosmetics kit to sell around town. David, excited at the possibility of selling enough to win a car, encourages Moira to do a lunch where they can sell to all the locals. Johnny, also concerned with bringing in an income, must find a way to get to Elmdale in the hope of collecting unemployment insurance, while Alexis thinks her new boyfriend Ted is too nice and tries to turn him into someone her family will respect.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny reluctantly agrees to speak at the funeral of a local man he doesn’t even know. Meanwhile, Alexis can’t seem to get close to Ted, as his love of animals gets in the way. David, on the other hand, finally gets up close and personal with Stevie, crossing the line from friend to friend with benefits.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: After spending a night together, David and Stevie’s relationship seems to be more then they had ever expected it to be. Meanwhile, Johnny and Moira invite themselves to Roland and Jocelyn’s annual Hawaiian bash and discover that they really aren’t that different. Hoping to show off their perfect relationship, Alexis and Ted throw a couples dinner party, but things go south when Alexis’s relationship with Mutt seems to be more than she says it is.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Dermot and Maria's wedding is just days away and tensions are running high in the Brown household. The groom is getting cold feet. His best man Buster can't find anything good to say about Dermot in his speech. To cap it all Agnes Brown is mystified as to why her daughter Cathy has given her a book on homosexuality. Is she trying to tell her mother that one of the family is gay? When Dermot's pre-marriage nerves get the better of him, he does what he always does when stressed. He locks himself in the cupboard. Father Quinn is bemused to have to give Maria and Dermot their pre-marriage talk in slightly unusual circumstances - with the mother of the groom in attendance and the groom in the cupboard. If Dermot won't reappear for the priest, how on earth is Agnes going to persuade him to come out in time for his wedding? Meanwhile, Agnes Brown goes on a hunt for the homosexual in the family. Eventually she realises who it is. With help from Cathy and a greetings card, Agnes reassures them, and herself, as only she can.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes Brown thinks everyone has gone mad. Father Quinn is losing his faith as fast as he loses parishioners. Newlyweds Dermot and Maria will not stop fighting. And Grandad has decided that he would like to witness his own funeral. He is keen to hear all the nice things people will say about him at his funeral while he is still alive to enjoy them. Maria tells Mrs Brown why she and Dermot have been arguing so much. She thinks that they should move out of the Brown household, but Dermot is resisting. He is also scheming with Buster to make some quick cash so that he can afford a first home for him and Maria. The Browns decide to make Grandad happy by faking his funeral, but their plans start to go awry when Mrs Brown mistakes two Mormons for the men who are supposed to be delivering Grandad's coffin. In the event, Grandad is horrified to find that no-one has a kind word to say about him after all, and the mourners get a shock when the corpse comes back to life. Only a quick-thinking Mrs Brown can save the day.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard battles medical receptionists – and a stubborn Ken – in order to secure Ken a doctor’s appointment on the same day that he is asked to impart his acting wisdom to the local am-dram group.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard secures an audition and embarks on a journey to track down the elusive Brian – Ken’s oldest friend, with whom he lost touch with years ago – in order to invite him to Ken’s surprise 75th birthday party.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard struggles with finding the right time to tell Ken his big news as the two of them spend the day touring Forres in search of the ideal spot to scatter Jean’s ashes.

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Magazine for the deaf community highlighting the latest issues.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: The competition steps up a gear as presenter Owain Wyn Evans, Olympic champion Greg Rutherford and Blue Peter’s Abby Cook return for day two of the quiz. Vernon Kay keeps control as host, while Paul Gorton leads an unreliable tour of Taunton, delivering fun facts with the odd curveball.

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Officers attending a call to a house where a woman has been punched in the face are confronted by a man with a large knife. There is a tense stand-off before they are able to cuff and arrest him. Stephen challenges a man arrested for a hate-motivated crime close to Belfast city centre. There are comical scenes as the Peelers try to herd cattle through the busy streets of west Belfast. Back in the city centre, Dave and Owen come across a group of drug users openly injecting cocaine in broad daylight, in a public square. As they arrive, they find one man still injecting. A tense stand-off ensues, and police describe how they have to deal with the drug abuse and homelessness crisis. Elsewhere, officers are called to reports of a street fight between two women. While the police are trying to establish order amid the chaos, one woman wrestles with them, biting Anna and spitting blood into Tom and Adam’s eyes. She’s taken into custody wearing a spit and bite guard. Stephen looks on as the visibly distressed woman is processed through the police system. He is deeply affected by what he sees and hears – the reality of frontline policing.

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: The group have been kicked out of the villa, and Zoe’s disappearance prompts a frantic search. Nat’s co-dependency with Jacob is challenged when unexpected guests trigger an awkward run-in with the local police. Unable to face up to her actions, Zoe is on a dangerous quest for answers. What is her connection to this island? Can she ever go back to who she was? And even if she could, is it more important to be happy or good?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty is in full summer mode - revamping and refreshing borders to keep the garden looking its best right through the season. He turns his attention to the cucurbit family, planting melons and squash, shares a simple trick to extend the flowering of hardy geraniums, and sows perennial seeds for spectacular colour next year. Arit Anderson heads to south east London to discover the striking transformation of a garden that was once a derelict space into a bold, contemporary community garden powered by creativity, collaboration and clever planting. Rachel de Thame explores how self-seeding plants can be carefully choreographed to create a beautifully natural, flowing design. There’s a passionate grower dedicated to the National Collection of penstemons, and in the Yorkshire Dales, a florist and lifelong gardener shares a deeply personal story of resilience - finding strength, purpose and renewed creativity through rebuilding a garden after a life-changing diagnosis.

programme.title: Marcel the Shell with Shoes On
programme.desc: When struggling film-maker Dean moves into a rental property, he quickly discovers he is not alone. Marcel, an adorable one-inch-tall shell, already lives there with his grandmother Connie and their pet lint Alan. Once part of a sprawling community of shells, Marcel and Connie live alone as the sole survivors of a mysterious tragedy. Taken with his unlikely new friend, Dean starts filming Marcel and his grandmother, igniting a meteoric rise to internet fame that changes their lives forever. But will Marcel’s newfound sense of possibility and a throng of superfans be enough to track down his long-lost family?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As Adam Frost’s brand new garden starts to take shape, it’s time to get some shrubs in the ground and construct a path. He also makes a start on planting up a potted herb garden and shares some ideas on what to grow against a wall, whether it be sunny or shady. At home in Devon, Carol Klein is mixing it up with flowers and edibles, where sweet peas and cosmos happily mingle with beetroot, basil and beans. And at West Dean Gardens in Chichester, Jamie Butterworth reveals the secrets of growing that border belle, the delphinium. In Birmingham, a lover of roses has created a garden full to brimming with beautiful blooms, while a gardener in Norwich shares her contemporary cottage garden that became a refuge when she needed it most.

programme.title: Wimbledon

programme.title: 50 American Music Icons at the BBC
programme.desc: As America celebrates 250 years of Independence, we celebrate 50 of the biggest names in modern American music and their performances over the years at the BBC. It’s a playlist of acts covering 70 years of classic hits, including the most influential and successful artists, bands and songwriters who made the journey from the US charts to international superstardom and also took time to appear on a UK audience's TV screens on their way. Featuring artists ranging from Beyonce and Bruce Springsteen, Ella Fitzgerald to Nirvana, Liberace to Johnny Cash, Louis Armstrong to Lady Gaga, and Carole King to Bruno Mars, this collection highlights the incredible range of genres and songs that US artists have given the world, and why so many of them are – to quote Tina Turner – simply the best.

In this episode, they find out all about the angle of the Earth's spin and how it affects lots of things, including our four seasons. Their friend and physicist Professor Brian Cox explains what the wild weather is like on other planets and what makes Earth the best place to live. And they discover how a massive space impact a long time ago made the Earth go wonky.

programme.title: BMX All Stars
programme.desc: The biggest races of the year are finally here for our BMX riders as they travel to Scotland to compete in the World Championships for their countries. After a brutally stormy last training session, they’ll be taking on the best riders in the world. It’s Adele’s first ever World Championships. And, to add to the pressure, she’s representing her home country Italy. She’s the only young female Italian rider here so can she make her country, club and family proud? UK number 1 Ti has dreams of becoming World number 1. At the last World Championships he went out in the quarters, but he’s been training hard and is determined to do better this time. But he’s up against the best riders in the world and when there’s a massive crash in the final, it threatens to jeopardise all that he has been working for. Can he get through and bag a spot on the podium? Featuring Olympians Kye Whyte and Quillan Isidore and BMXer Tian Isidore. Narrated by Ashley Walters.
